name: Alaró Castle Loop Trail
name_original: Castillo de Alaró — circular por el Paraje Natural de la Serra de Tramuntana
description: Hike the moderate 8.4-mile Castillo de Alaró circular trail in Serra de Tramuntana, featuring castle ruins, waterfalls, and panoramic views.
region_intro: Hiking around Bunyola offers access to the diverse landscapes of Mallorca's Serra de Tramuntana mountains, a UNESCO World Heritage Site. The region is characterized by dramatic peaks, lush valleys, and Mediterranean forests primarily composed of holm oaks and pines. Natural features include prominent peaks like Penyal d'Honor, tranquil springs, and the Barranc de Coanegra gorge. These varied terrains provide a range of experiences for outdoor enthusiasts. Best hiking trails around Bunyola The most popular hiking route is Alaró Castle Loop Trail, an 8.4 miles (13.6 km) trail that takes 4 hours 27 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers panoramic views of the Serra de Tramuntana from Castell d'Alaró. Another top favourite among local hikers is Bunyola Steps – View from Penyal d'Honor loop from Bunyola, a moderate 8.3 miles (13.3 km) path. This trail leads through Mediterranean forests to the summit of Penyal d'Honor, providing sweeping views. Local hikers also love the Raixa Estate and Gardens – Bunyola Steps loop from Bunyola, a 6.1 miles (9.8 km) trail leading through the historic Raixa estate and its gardens, often completed in about 2 hours 38 minutes. Hiking around Bunyola is defined by mountain paths, Mediterranean forests, and historic estates. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging ascents. The routes in Bunyola are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 2290 reviews. More than 9900 hikers have used komoot to explore Bunyola's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Bunyola?
A: Bunyola offers a wide variety of hiking experiences, with over 90 routes available. These range from easy strolls to more challenging ascents, catering to different skill levels.

Q: Are there easy or family-friendly hiking options in Bunyola?
A: Yes, Bunyola is well-suited for families and those seeking easier walks. There are nearly 30 easy routes available. The walk to &lt;strong&gt;Salt des Freu&lt;/strong&gt;, a beautiful natural waterfall, is particularly accessible and ideal for families, especially during winter and spring when water flow is higher. The excursion from Raixa to Raixeta is also a pleasant, easy walk.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Bunyola?
A: Hiking in Bunyola immerses you in the diverse landscapes of Mallorca's Serra de Tramuntana. You'll traverse beautiful Mediterranean forests primarily composed of holm oaks and pines, as well as olive and almond groves. Expect dramatic peaks, lush valleys, and stunning coastal views from higher elevations. The &lt;strong&gt;Sa Comuna forest&lt;/strong&gt; is particularly noted for its abundance of pine trees and holm oaks, offering shady paths.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes around Bunyola?
A: Yes, many routes in Bunyola are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. A popular moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24833" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bunyola Steps – View from Penyal d'Honor loop from Bunyola&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through Mediterranean forests to the summit of Penyal d'Honor. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/508177" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Raixa Estate and Gardens – Bunyola Steps loop from Bunyola&lt;/a&gt;, exploring historic grounds.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions I can visit while hiking near Bunyola?
A: Several interesting landmarks can be incorporated into your hikes. You can visit the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/240092" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alaró Castle&lt;/a&gt;, which offers panoramic views of the Serra de Tramuntana. The impressive &lt;strong&gt;Raixa estate&lt;/strong&gt;, with its monumental staircase and large pond, is also a cultural asset with beautiful gardens, accessible via routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/508177" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Raixa Estate and Gardens – Bunyola Steps loop from Bunyola&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Bunyola?
A: The hiking routes in Bunyola are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 2290 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse terrain, from mountain paths to Mediterranean forests and historic estates, and the stunning panoramic views from peaks like Penyal d'Honor and Castell d'Alaró.

Q: Are there any challenging or advanced hiking routes in the Bunyola area?
A: For experienced hikers, Bunyola offers 11 difficult routes. One such challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/11982109" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Raixa Estate and Gardens – Raixa Reservoir loop from Raixa&lt;/a&gt;, which covers a significant distance and elevation gain, providing a rewarding experience for those seeking a strenuous adventure.

Q: When is the best time of year to go hiking in Bunyola?
A: Hiking in Bunyola is particularly enjoyable in spring and autumn. During these seasons, the weather is mild, making it ideal for exploring the trails. The ascent to Penyal d'Honor, for instance, is highly recommended during these times. Winter also offers unique beauty, especially for routes featuring waterfalls like Salt des Freu, which are more impressive with higher water flow.

Q: Can I find any natural springs or viewpoints along the trails?
A: Yes, the area is dotted with natural springs such as &lt;strong&gt;Font des Pixarells&lt;/strong&gt; and &lt;strong&gt;Font de la Comuna&lt;/strong&gt;, providing tranquil spots for rest and refreshment. You'll also discover numerous viewpoints like &lt;strong&gt;Puig de n'Alí&lt;/strong&gt; and &lt;strong&gt;Comellar&lt;/strong&gt;, which offer sweeping vistas of the Tramuntana mountains and the forest's interior.

Q: Are there any historical elements or ethnological remains to discover on the trails?
A: Absolutely. The extensive &lt;strong&gt;Sa Comuna de Bunyola&lt;/strong&gt; forest, in particular, features routes with significant historical value. You can find ethnological remains such as old bread and lime ovens, charcoal shacks, and silos, offering a glimpse into the region's past and the traditional livelihoods of its inhabitants.

Q: What is the Barranc de Coanegra and can I visit it?
A: The &lt;strong&gt;Barranc de Coanegra&lt;/strong&gt; is a hidden gem in Bunyola, known for canyoning. It features a magnificent 25-meter waterfall at &lt;strong&gt;Salt de's Freu&lt;/strong&gt; and offers opportunities for jumps, slides, and rappelling within a vibrant Mediterranean forest. While some parts are for canyoning, the area around Salt des Freu is accessible for hikers, especially the easy route to the waterfall.
tips: Impressive rock breakthrough. Wonderful view of the valley to Orient.

The last ascent to the castle takes place on rustic steps. As you work your way up, the view spreads in front of you. When it rains, the field stones can be slippery and it is then advisable to wear hiking boots.
From the Castell d'Alaró you have a fantastic view over the island, on a clear day you can see the Bay of Alcudia.

A few meters above the mountain hut you have an incredible panorama. In the west, the highest peaks of the Tramuntana mountains rise up and in the north you can see the unique table mountain Puig s'Alcadena. With the Puig d'Alaro, on whose summit plateau you are currently standing, it forms the twin mesas of the Tramuntana mountains.
A fantastic route, mainly because of the view, but also because of the many old olive trees.

faqs.question: What is the overall difficulty and terrain like for this circular hike?
faqs.answer: This hike is generally rated as moderate. The terrain is quite varied, including rocky paths, stone steps (like the famous Pas de s'Escaleta), tracks through olive groves, and some paved sections. As you approach the castle, it becomes rockier and steeper. Be aware that some descents can be challenging, overgrown, and potentially slippery, especially after rain.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The primary destination is the historic Castell d'Alaró, offering incredible 360-degree views. Along the way, you'll pass through beautiful olive groves, experience the Stone Steps to Castell d'Alaró, and enjoy panoramic vistas from spots like the View of Puig s'Alcadena from the Mirador de l'Orengar. You might also encounter the Salto des Freu Falls and the s'Estaló Pass.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to hike to Castillo de Alaró?
faqs.answer: The ideal times to visit are during spring (March to June) and fall (September to November). During these seasons, the weather is pleasant, wildflowers are in bloom, and you can avoid the intense summer heat and crowds. Winter can also be beautiful, but some sections might be slippery after rain.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start the hike, and are there public transport options?
faqs.answer: Most hikers start from the town of Alaró, where parking is available. Specific parking areas can be found near the trailhead. While the research doesn't detail specific public transport to Alaró, it's generally accessible within Mallorca. Consider checking local bus schedules for routes to Alaró.

faqs.question: Is the Castillo de Alaró — circular by the Serra de Tramuntana Natural Park trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The provided information does not explicitly state whether the trail is dog-friendly. However, as it's within the Serra de Tramuntana Natural Park, it's advisable to keep dogs on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure their safety on varied terrain. Always check local regulations before bringing your dog.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to hike this trail or enter the Serra de Tramuntana Natural Park?
faqs.answer: No, there are typically no permits or entrance fees required to hike this trail or to access the Serra de Tramuntana Natural Park itself. However, always respect the natural environment and any local signage regarding access or regulations.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities for food, drinks, or accommodation along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, near the summit of Castell d'Alaró, you'll find the Hospedería del Castillo de Alaró. This refuge offers coffee, cold drinks, sandwiches, and even lodging for overnight stays. Supplies for the refuge are famously brought up by donkeys! Additionally, the renowned Es Verger Restaurant, famous for its lamb shoulder, is often incorporated into some hiking routes in the area.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other paths. You'll find sections that are part of the Ruta de Pedra en Sec, the camí des Freu, and various segments of the Camí d'Orient al Castell d'Alaró. It also briefly touches upon the long-distance GR-221 trail in places.

faqs.question: What kind of historical significance does the Castillo de Alaró hold?
faqs.answer: The Castell d'Alaró is one of Mallorca's three rock castles, dating back to 902 AD. It's a significant cultural landmark, symbolizing freedom in Mallorcan history. The ruins include fortress walls and the Torre d'Homenatge. There's also an option to explore the Prison of the Moors and the Cave of Saint Anthony, adding deeper historical context to your hike.

faqs.question: Are there any specific challenges or insider tips for hiking this trail?
faqs.answer: While generally moderate, some descents can be difficult, hard to follow, and potentially dangerous due to overgrown paths and slippery conditions, especially after rain. Some hikers recommend alternative routes for these sections. Be prepared for rocky and steep ascents, particularly the final stretch to the castle. Good hiking shoes with ankle support are highly recommended.

Olive Grove Path to Castell d'Alaró
